# README

## Title 
Estimating the time-varying transmission rate of Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza virus in Newfoundland seabirds using a SEIRD model 

---

## Summary

The transmission rate of Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza (HPAI) virus in wild birds is difficult to estimate and can vary over time due to species, spatial or seasonal differences. The purpose of this research is to develop a SEIRD (Susceptible-Exposed-Infectious-Recovered-Dead) compartmental model to simulate the spread of HPAI virus among NFLD seabirds in 2022 and estimate the time-varying transmission rate. These estimates can inform what types of values and temporal patterns are reasonable for modelling future scenarios. 

---

## Repository overview
This repository contains the code and data used to estimate the time-varying transmission rate of HPAI virus in NFLD seabirds using our SEIRD model.
---

### Code version
- R version 4.4.1

### Packages versions
- sf (1.0.23)
- patchwork (1.3.2)
- dplyr (1.1.4)
- ggplot2 (4.0.1)
- ggtext (0.1.2)
- scales (1.4.0)
- ggrepel (0.9.6)
- grid (4.4.1)
- macpan2 (3.5.1)
- tidyverse (2.0.0)
- zoo (1.8.14)
- stringr (1.6.0)
- splines (4.4.1)
- viridis (0.6.5)
- ggspatial (1.1.10)
- rnaturalearth (1.1.0)
- rnaturalearthdata (1.0.0.9000)
- rnaturalearthhires (1.0.0.9000)
- gganimate (1.0.11)
- gifski (1.32.0.2)
- transformr (0.1.5)
- cowplot (1.2.0)
- cli (3.6.5.9000)
- purrr (1.2.0)
- geosphere (1.6.5)
- reshape2 (1.4.5)
- lme4 (1.1-38)
- shellpipes (0.1.0)
---

### File descriptions

### Code
The `Code/` directory contains the following scripts:
- `NFLD.R`: Spatial distribution of seabird mortality associated with the 2022 HPAI virus outbreak
- `Delay_distribution.R`: Negative binomial delay distribution for Northern Gannet aerial survey observations
- `Data_interpolation.R`: Aggregation and linear interpolation of cumulative mortality observations to produce daily mortality time series for SEIRD model fitting
- `SEIRD.R`: SEIRD model for the spread of HPAI virus in Newfoundland seabirds
- `SEIRD_underreporting.R`: SEIRD model for the spread of HPAI virus in Newfoundland seabirds with underreporting scenarios
- `Connectivity_matrix.R`: Inter-patch connectivity matrix
- `Labrador.R`: Proposed patches for Labrador

### Data
The `Data/` directory contains the following files:
- `Data S1. Reported mortalities and morbidities in eastern Canada 2022.csv`: Reported wild bird mortalities and morbidities associated with the 2022 HPAI virus outbreak in eastern Canada
- `Important Bird and Biodiversity Areas of Canada Zones importantes pour la conservation des oiseaux et de la biodiversité du Can.kml`: Spatial boundaries of Important Bird and Biodiversity Areas used for mapping and patch delineation
- `NFLD_mortalities_7patch_with_backdistributed_gannets_NB.csv`: Newfoundland and Labrador seabird mortality dataset used for SEIRD model fitting, including negative binomial delay distribution for Northern Gannet aerial survey observations
- `dat.rds`: Daily interpolated cumulative mortality dataset generated by `Data_interpolation.R` and used as input for the SEIRD models
---

## Instructions to run the software
1. Install R version 4.4.1.
2. Install required R packages listed above.
3. Set the working directory to the repository root.
4. Ensure the required data files are present in the `Data/` directory (see File descriptions for full list of data files).
5. Run the scripts in the following order:
   - `NFLD.R`
   - `Delay_distribution.R`
   - `Data_interpolation.R`
   - `SEIRD.R`
   - `Connectivity_matrix.R`
   - `Labrador.R`
